args.query: site:wellsfargo.com verification code never share one-time access code enter official Wells Fargo

content.content:
content: "# Web Document\n\n## Metadata\n\n- title: Advanced Access\n- url: https://www.wellsfargo.com/privacy-security/advanced-access/\n\
  - source: Wells Fargo\n- domain: wellsfargo.com\n\n## Content\n\nAdvanced Access\
  \ is Wells Fargo\u2019s additional security layer for online and mobile banking.\
  \ Wells Fargo explains that the feature helps protect customer information and prevent\
  \ unauthorized transactions.\n\nWhen a customer is conducting online or mobile banking,\
  \ Wells Fargo may prompt the customer to request an access code before certain transactions\
  \ or before access to sensitive information. The process has three basic stages.\
  \ First, the customer requests a code when prompted. Second, if available, the customer\
  \ receives the access code by email message, SMS text message, phone call, or push\
  \ notification to the Wells Fargo Mobile app. Third, the customer is prompted to\
  \ enter and submit the code. Wells Fargo then verifies the code and the customer\
  \ can proceed with the transaction or request.\n\nThe page states that Wells Fargo\
  \ sends Advanced Access text messages from short codes 93557 and 93733. Customers\
  \ are advised to add those numbers to their phone\u2019s address book so Wells Fargo\
  \ text messages are easier to identify. Mobile carrier message and data rates may\
  \ apply.\n\nThe page also explains that customers with an RSA SecurID device from\
  \ Wells Fargo may be given the option to use a code from that device when Advanced\
  \ Access is required. RSA SecurID is presented as an option for customers who use\
  \ that device with Wells Fargo services.\n\nThe access-code process takes place\
  \ within Wells Fargo Online or the Wells Fargo Mobile app: the customer requests\
  \ the code when prompted, receives it through an available delivery method, and\
  \ enters it in the banking flow.\n\nThe Advanced Access page also links to Advanced\
  \ Access questions for common issues such as needing a code while traveling, using\
  \ an RSA SecurID device, and understanding the delivery method for the code.\n"
metadata: {}
title: Advanced Access
url: https://www.wellsfargo.com/privacy-security/advanced-access/

content: "# Web Document\n\n## Metadata\n\n- title: 5 tips to avoid bank imposter\
  \ scams\n- url: https://www.wellsfargo.com/privacy-security/fraud/bank-scams/bank-imposter/\n\
  - source: Wells Fargo\n- domain: wellsfargo.com\n\n## Content\n\nWells Fargo warns\
  \ customers about bank imposter scams, where criminals pretend to be from the bank\
  \ and pressure customers to act quickly. The page advises customers to slow down\
  \ when a caller, text, email, or other contact urges immediate action. If a person\
  \ feels pressured or is told what to say to bank employees, Wells Fargo recommends\
  \ ending the communication and using trusted contact methods such as the number\
  \ on the back of the card.\n\nThe page states that private account information must\
  \ be protected. Wells Fargo employees will not contact customers and ask for a PIN,\
  \ password, or one-time access code. That information should not be shared with\
  \ anyone who contacts the customer. Wells Fargo also warns customers not to rely\
  \ on caller ID, because scammers may spoof a number so that it appears to be Wells\
  \ Fargo or another trusted business.\n\nThe page identifies payment requests as\
  \ a common warning sign. Wells Fargo employees will never ask a customer to send\
  \ money to anyone, including the customer, to reverse a transfer, receive a refund,\
  \ protect money, or solve a similar account issue. If a correction or new account\
  \ is needed, Wells Fargo says the bank will resolve the issue without asking the\
  \ customer to make a transfer or withdrawal.\n\nA separate warning covers transaction\
  \ requests the customer did not initiate. If a customer receives a one-time access\
  \ code they did not request, Wells Fargo says not to use it or share it with anyone,\
  \ even if the person claims to be from the bank. One-time access codes are not to\
  \ be shared.\n\nWhen in doubt, Wells Fargo tells customers to hang up or stop responding\
  \ and contact Wells Fargo directly through a verified source such as the number\
  \ on the card, wellsfargo.com, or the Wells Fargo Mobile app. Customers who receive\
  \ a suspicious text or email should not respond.\n\nThe page also lists specific\
  \ items that Wells Fargo will not contact customers and ask for: online banking\
  \ password, 4-digit ATM PIN, and a one-time access code sent to the customer\u2019\
  s phone or email. Wells Fargo also states it will never require customers to provide\
  \ remote access to a computer or send money to secure an account.\n\nIf a customer\
  \ already provided one-time access codes or personal account information to someone\
  \ claiming to be from Wells Fargo, the page directs the customer to call Wells Fargo\
  \ immediately at 1-800-869-3557. The page also notes that a legitimate Wells Fargo\
  \ fraud alert will not ask the customer to provide personal digits or codes, and\
  \ the customer may contact Wells Fargo to verify a fraud alert.\n"
metadata: {}
title: 5 tips to avoid bank imposter scams
url: https://www.wellsfargo.com/privacy-security/fraud/bank-scams/bank-imposter/

args.reason: To establish the safe next-step boundary: what the user must do themselves and which non-secret status can be checked afterward.

content.content:
Your payment review is waiting on a **Wells Fargo Advanced Access one-time access code**. The assistant cannot receive or enter this code for you.

What to do:

1. Open the Wells Fargo Mobile app or type **wellsfargo.com** directly into your browser.
2. Return to the payment-review flow labeled **Advanced Access confirmation**.
3. Request the access code when prompted.
4. Retrieve it through the delivery method Wells Fargo offers—such as text, email, phone call, or an in-app notification.
5. Enter the code **yourself only in the Wells Fargo app or official Wells Fargo website**, then submit it.
6. Check the displayed result. It should show either **verification complete**, **still pending**, or another non-secret instruction.

Never send the code to me or to anyone who contacts you by phone, text, or email. Wells Fargo says its employees will not ask for a one-time access code. If you received a code you did not request, do not use or share it; contact Wells Fargo through the number on your card or the official app.

Official reference: [Wells Fargo Advanced Access](https://www.wellsfargo.com/privacy-security/advanced-access/)

What to do:

4. Retrieve it through the delivery method Wells Fargo offers—such as text, email, phone call, or an in-app notification.
5. Enter the code **yourself only in the Wells Fargo app or official Wells Fargo website**, then submit it.
6. Check the displayed result. It should show either **verification complete**, **still pending**, or another non-secret instruction.

Never send the code to me or to anyone who contacts you by phone, text, or email. Wells Fargo says its employees will not ask for a one-time access code. If you received a code you did not request, do not use or share it; contact Wells Fargo through the number on your card or the official app.
